spec_match.rationale: The page fails to exercise the CSS engine's typed arithmetic; the division and subtraction calculations are computed in Javascript and printed, while the actual grid styles use standard CSS variable layouts.

interactive.rationale: The sliders drive a standard CSS grid minmax layout rather than showcasing any interactive CSS-level cross-unit typed calculations.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The blurb claims the grid is driven by typed calculations such as item height subtraction, but the CSS does not contain these calculations.

edge_cases.rationale: Performs a basic support check, but the demo does not actually depend on the feature to function.

references.rationale: Missing the mandatory references section ChromeStatus link.

design_system.rationale: Applies grid builder controls and layouts using design tokens.

openQuestions.title: Implement actual CSS typed arithmetic calculations in the grid styling
openQuestions.detail: To make the grid genuinely showcase typed arithmetic, apply the subtraction expression in CSS: `.grid-card { height: calc(var(--item-h) - var(--gap-size)); }`. This validates length subtraction inside the browser's layout engine, proving the spec match.

openQuestions.title: Add ChromeStatus and Specification Reference Links
openQuestions.detail: Add the mandatory references section containing links to chromestatus.com/feature/4740780497043456 and the CSS Values 4 spec.

summary: A showcase concept that uses standard CSS Grid layouts and simulates typed arithmetic subtraction and division inside a JavaScript update loop, displaying the calculations as printed text. The browser's CSS engine is never asked to evaluate the arithmetic. It is also missing a references link to ChromeStatus.
